title: Mulanje Hotels & Lodges: Where to Stay for Your Mountain Trip
meta_title: Mulanje Hotels & Lodges: Where to Stay (2026)
meta_description: A guide to the best Mulanje hotels, lodges and guesthouses for hikers and travellers — from mountain huts to a boutique tea estate retreat nearby.
author: The Thyolo House
excerpt: Finding the right place to stay can make or break a Mulanje trip. From simple mountain huts to colonial-era guesthouses and a boutique Italian-run retreat on a century-old tea estate, here's every option worth knowing about.
content:
Blog post written. Here's a summary:

**File:** `src/content/blog/mulanje-hotels-where-to-stay.json`

**Stats:**
- **~2,000 words** of SEO-optimized HTML content
- **Target keyword** "mulanje hotels" appears in first paragraph + 4 more times naturally
- **7 H2 sections** following the outline exactly
- **5 images** — forest-view, garden-view, main-house, outdoor-dining, heritage-suite with contextual captions
- **3 internal links** — `/blog/mulanje-tea-estates-guide`, `/blog/story-of-conforzi-tea-estate`, `/blog/mulanje-mountain-huts-booking-guide`
- **The Thyolo House** mentioned 4 times naturally (boutique section, multi-day trek advice, day-hiking option, comfort recommendation)
- **WhatsApp CTA** + email in closing paragraph
- **Research facts used:** UNESCO 2025 inscription, Kara O Mula details (27 rooms, $60–115), Hapuwani (40 rooms, AC, pool/gym), Mount Mulanje Stopover, AfricaWildTruck, all 10 huts with named examples, MMCT fees, peak/budget months
- **Practical info:** distances, prices, road conditions, transport options, cash/permit/guide tips
- Clean HTML, no wrapper elements
